title: India Auto Demand Monitor
subtitle: Official-first dashboard for listed Indian auto and auto-component investors.

cards.label: Derived EV Penetration
cards.detail: Derived from FADA category fuel mix; excludes low-confidence categories.

cards.label: PV Inventory
cards.detail: Healthy improvement from 53-55 days in Oct 2025 to 27-29 days now.

cards.label: PV Retail / Wholesale
cards.detail: Retail from FADA vs wholesale from SIAM; channel timing still matters.

cards.label: Dealer Growth View
cards.detail: 67.3% expect growth over the next three months.

source_badges.detail: Vehicle Retail Data collated as on 02.03.26 in collaboration with Ministry of Road Transport & Highways, Government of India and gathered from 1,459 out of 1,464 RTOs. Telangana figures are excluded.

Monthly performance page provides production totals and domestic sales for PV, 2W and 3W. SIAM notes that BMW, Mercedes, JLR and Volvo Auto data are excluded from regular PV reporting, with Tata Motors domestic PV data included only in the monthly note.
ACMA notes that tyres, batteries, paints and consumables are excluded from industry scope; EV component sales to domestic OEMs are included under OEM supply figures, excluding batteries.

source_meta.name: FADA
source_meta.note: Vehicle Retail Data collated as on 02.03.26 in collaboration with Ministry of Road Transport & Highways, Government of India and gathered from 1,459 out of 1,464 RTOs. Telangana figures are excluded.

bullets: February 2026 was the best-ever February for 2W, 3W, CV, PV, tractors and total retail.
Rural demand stayed strong, especially in tractors and passenger vehicles.
PV inventory reduced further toward the healthy 21-day benchmark, indicating better wholesale-retail alignment.
Dealer sentiment for March remained cautiously optimistic, supported by festivals and year-end buying.

registration.title: Registration Lens
registration.hidden_reason: No validated Vahan import found in data/vahan.

source_meta.name: SIAM
source_meta.note: Monthly performance page provides production totals and domestic sales for PV, 2W and 3W. SIAM notes that BMW, Mercedes, JLR and Volvo Auto data are excluded from regular PV reporting, with Tata Motors domestic PV data included only in the monthly note.

components.title: Components

source_meta.name: ACMA
source_meta.note: ACMA notes that tyres, batteries, paints and consumables are excluded from industry scope; EV component sales to domestic OEMs are included under OEM supply figures, excluding batteries.

components.headline: Indian auto component industry clocks turnover of Rs 6.73 lakh crore in FY25.
insights: Domestic OEM demand remained the largest growth driver, supported by about 8% growth in vehicle production.
Exports stayed healthy despite geopolitical disruptions, with North America still the largest market and Asia the fastest-growing region.
Aftermarket demand stayed firm on a larger used-vehicle base, formalisation of repairs, and rising e-commerce penetration.
ACMA specifically flags rare-earth magnet availability as a strategic watchpoint for EV and mobility manufacturing.

Broad 2W exposure plus 3W participation and e-2W optionality.
Strong listed proxy for both motorcycles and 3W passenger/goods demand.
Listed 3W niche exposure with potential benefit from last-mile demand.
Battery beneficiary from EV penetration and replacement demand.
Battery and energy-storage proxy with EV transition leverage.
EV drivetrain content proxy tied to electrification mix gains.
CV cycle plus component export and EV platform optionality.
Broad content supplier across lighting, switches and new mobility programs.

Bus and electric mobility exposure with public transport optionality.
Electric bus proxy rather than broad auto demand beta.
Small but relevant e-mobility and last-mile ecosystem proxy.

insights.title: Segment speed
insights.body: Tractors were the fastest-growing retail category in Feb 2026 at +36.35% YoY, while Construction Equipment stayed weakest at -1.22% YoY.

insights.title: OEM share shifts
insights.body: In PV retail, Tata Motors gained the most share at +1.68 pp YoY, while Hyundai Motor India lost -1.03 pp. In 2W, the biggest gainer was Honda Motorcycle & Scooter India at +1.11 pp, while Hero MotoCorp was the sharpest loser at -1.51 pp.

insights.title: Retail vs wholesale
insights.body: PV retail ran at 94.5% of wholesale in February 2026, versus 90.9% for 2W. The tighter PV ratio sits alongside FADA's 27-29 day inventory signal, suggesting channel conditions have improved materially.

insights.title: EV transition
insights.body: Derived EV penetration in February 2026 was 8.05% of retail volume. The strongest EV mix remains in Three-Wheelers at 56.70%, while PV EV share stayed below 4%, which keeps battery, drivetrain and power-electronics suppliers more leveraged than OEM headline units alone.

insights.title: Component read-across
insights.body: ACMA's FY25 review still points to a healthy ancillary backdrop: OEM supplies grew 10% YoY, exports grew 8%, and aftermarket grew 6%. That supports continued interest in export-capable, value-added suppliers even if near-term OEM mix shifts month to month.
